name: Circle Community Platform API
what_it_does: Circle online community platform REST API for creators, coaches, and brands to build and manage paid or free member communities with spaces, events, courses, live streams, and member engagement tools. Enables AI agents to manage community member management and onboarding for membership automation, handle space and group creation for community organization automation, access post and content creation for community content automation, retrieve member activity and engagement analytics for community health automation, manage event creation and live stream scheduling for community programming automation, handle course module and curriculum management for educational community automation, access payment and subscription management for membership monetization automation, retrieve member search and segmentation for targeted communication automation, manage notifications and digest email for member engagement automation, and integrate Circle with Zapier, MemberStack, Stripe, and email platforms for end-to-end community management automation.
use_cases: Onboarding community members from membership management agents
Creating community content from engagement automation agents
Managing courses from educational community agents
Tracking engagement from community analytics agents
not_for: Large-scale social networks and public forums (use Discourse or Reddit-style platforms)
Customer support communities (use Zendesk Community or Freshdesk Forum)
B2B enterprise community management at scale (use Khoros or Higher Logic)
best_when: A creator, coach, or brand wanting AI agents to automate community member management, content creation, course delivery, event scheduling, and engagement tracking through Circle's creator-focused community platform.
avoid_when: MEMBER ONBOARDING FLOW REQUIRES CIRCLE PAYMENT IF PAID COMMUNITY: Circle handles payment for paid communities; automated member provisioning for paid communities must go through Circle's payment system; automated direct member addition bypassing Circle payment creates members without subscription payment records causing access inconsistency. SPACE PERMISSION GRANULARITY FOR CONTENT ORGANIZATION: Circle spaces have member access settings (all members, specific groups, paid tiers); automated content organization must match space access settings with intended audience; automated content creation in wrong-access-level space creates content accessible to wrong member segment without error. API RATE LIMITS FOR HIGH-VOLUME COMMUNITY OPERATIONS: Circle API rate limits may constrain high-volume automated community operations (bulk member import, mass post creation); automated community setup workflows must throttle API calls; automated bulk operations without rate limit awareness create 429 errors requiring retry logic.

auth.notes: Circle uses API key and OAuth for integrations. REST API with JSON. San Francisco, California HQ. Founded 2020 by Sid Yadav, Rudy Santino, and Andrew Guttormsen. Backed by Tiger Global, Bessemer Venture Partners, Andreessen Horowitz ($85M+ raised). Products: Community spaces, events, live streams, courses, payments, member analytics. Integrations: Zapier, MemberStack, ConvertKit, Klaviyo, Stripe. GDPR. SOC2. Serves creators, coaches, and brands. Competes with Mighty Networks, Kajabi Community, and Tribe for creator communities.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free plan (1 community, limited members, basic features, Circle branding)
pricing.paid_tiers: Basic ($49/mo), Professional ($99/mo), Business ($219/mo), Enterprise ($399/mo); annual discount; + platform revenue share on member payments
pricing.notes: San Francisco CA. a16z/Bessemer backed. Free tier + paid plans. Revenue share on paid communities. GDPR, SOC2.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits per API key; member and content queries throttled; webhooks for member join, post create, and payment events

known_agent_gotchas: COMMUNITY ID REQUIRED IN ALL API REQUESTS: Circle API is community-scoped; all requests require community ID in URL path or request body; automated multi-community management must maintain community ID registry; automated requests without correct community ID create 404 or access denied errors regardless of valid API key

MEMBER INVITATION vs DIRECT ADD DISTINCTION: Circle allows member invitation (sends email) or direct add (bypasses email for SSO/automation); automated member onboarding must choose correct method; automated invitation workflow for SSO-based member onboarding creates unnecessary invitation email for users already authenticated via SSO

POST SPACE ASSIGNMENT FOR CONTENT ROUTING: Circle posts must be assigned to specific space; automated content creation must specify correct space ID for content routing; automated content generation without explicit space assignment creates orphaned post not visible in community space navigation

WEBHOOK DELIVERY VERIFICATION FOR MEMBER EVENTS: Circle webhooks include signature for verification; automated member onboarding that triggers on member_joined webhook must verify signature before provisioning; unverified webhook processing creates security risk from spoofed membership events

COURSE SECTION vs LESSON vs MODULE HIERARCHY: Circle courses use Section → Lesson hierarchy; automated curriculum management must maintain correct hierarchy nesting; automated course content import must sequence section creation before lesson creation with correct parent section reference
agent_readiness.security_notes: Community platform. SOC2, GDPR. API key/OAuth2. US. Community member, content, and payment data.

security_score_components.security_notes: Community platform. SOC2, GDPR. API key/OAuth2. US. Member, content, and payment data.
